DIVERGE region code: first byte differs at offset 0x35 (guest 0x10035) -- 00 vs 40

Status: SSHD reaches the FirstRsxWrite checkpoint at PPU step
14,352,589 (was 14,341,466 in the prior fixture, was 14,109,359
before the syscall-number fixes in commit 8033644). The +11,123
delta from the prior fixture is the post-Phase-29 trajectory;
SSHD does not call cellSaveDataAutoLoad, so the shift comes from
the central resume dispatcher refactor and small ordering nudges
in the wake path rather than any direct AutoLoad code. The
checkpoint is still reached deterministically (bit-identical
across two bench-boot runs, `agreement: 0.82%`).

The reported divergence is at offset 0x35 of the code region: the
low byte of the ELF header's `e_ehsize` field. CellGov reads 0x00,
RPCS3 reads 0x40 (= 64, the spec value). The same divergence class
that the WipEout report tracks under "ELF header metadata" -- the
header bytes captured by CellGov's loaded image differ from the
in-memory SELF that RPCS3 mmaps.

This bug pre-dates the syscall-number fix; the prior SSHD report
called the code region "byte-identical (0x7c4f68 bytes match)" but
that was generated against a different cellgov.json (Apr 15) and
the result no longer holds against the post-fix observation.

`compare-observations` stops at the first divergence, so the data
region's HLE OPD layout difference (previously documented at
offset 0xbf39) was not re-confirmed by this run.

Verdict: not strictly equivalent at the byte level. The diverging
header bytes are not read by the executing code; classification
remains "PS3-wide, non-semantic" in line with WipEout's similar
note. A future cleanup should re-classify by walking past the
ELF-header divergence and confirming that the only remaining
differences are the documented OPD layout class.

Re-confirmed at the current branch tip: step count is 14,352,589
(deterministic across runs). SSHD's CRT0/init path does not
exercise the PPU corner cases earlier correctness fixes addressed
(XER.SO propagation, FP fused-multiply-add, super-pair invalidation
widening, etc.), nor does it call cellSaveDataAutoLoad, so the
+11,123 step shift relative to the prior fixture is small but
real. The trajectory is bit-identical run-to-run.
